breaking.title: West Asia war LIVE: U.S. strikes Iran for 10th consecutive night as Tehran strikes tanker in the Strait of Hormuz
breaking.summary: U.S. strikes come as ships attacked; nearly 100 U.S. injuries since early July; Yemen rebels threaten attacks on other Mideast waterway; a glimmer of hope for diplomacy

breaking.title: Dream voyage ends in tragedy for Keralite as missile strike kills merchant navy officer weeks after wedding plans
breaking.summary: Akhil Joyan, a 26-year-old merchant navy officer from Kerala, was killed in a Russian missile strike on the MV Golden Leo near Odessa, Ukraine, just 40 days after returning to sea with plans to marry his fiancée next April.

breaking.title: Methane Gas Leak Triggered Sikkim Tunnel Collapse That Killed 10: Sources
breaking.summary: The workers feared trapped in the tinnel belong to different parts of the country.

breaking.title: Strike on Ukraine-bound gas tanker in Black Sea wounds three
breaking.summary: The Black Sea has been a regular theatre of confrontation between Ukraine and Russia since the Kremlin's full-scale invasion began in 2022

breaking.title: Farmers stopped at Punjab-Haryana border en route to Delhi rally against India-U.S. trade deal
breaking.summary: Farmers wanted to peacefully go to Delhi and participate in the rally but the BJP-led Haryana government has stopped them by erecting barricades, Sarwan Singh Pandher, farmer leader and coordinator of the Desh Bachao Morcha, says

breaking.title: Pothundy double murder case: a death penalty verdict with rare focus on rehabilitating victims’ family
breaking.summary: Court directs District Legal Services Authority, Palakkad, to initiate rehabilitation measures for orphaned children of the victims; legal experts say such a specific direction was rare in criminal judgments

breaking.title: Russia in touch with New Delhi over killing of Indian ship crew in Black Sea, Kremlin says
breaking.summary: New Delhi summoned the Russian chargé d’affaires over the ‌strike on Golden Leo, a Guinea-Bissau-flagged ship manned by a ‌crew from India ​and Syria

breaking.title: American oystercatchers were wiped out in New England, now their numbers are 45% higher along US coasts; here's how
breaking.summary: Once considered extinct in New England, the American oystercatcher is experiencing a remarkable resurgence thanks to dedicated conservation efforts. These initiatives focus on safeguarding their nesting environments and raising community awareness. Techniques such as banding chicks and preventing disturbances in ground-nesting sites have proven successful. Their resilience, coupled with federal safety measures for related shorebirds, makes their comeback a testament to effective conservation in the face of climate change challenges.
